meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code for AI builders.
content.answer_block: Overcoming the 'no portfolio' trap requires shipping production-grade AI features fast. By integrating Claude Code with Antigravity, you automate boilerplate architecture and focus on high-value logic. This workflow builds a robust, deployable project in hours, proving your technical competency to employers through tangible, complex, and professional-grade codebases.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank repository, the silence of a missing portfolio echoing louder than your ambitions. Every job description demands experience you haven't been given the chance to earn, creating a paralyzing loop of 'no experience, no job, no experience.' The industry doesn't care about your GPA; it cares about your ability to architect, debug, and ship.

Today, we break that cycle. We are moving beyond simple tutorials and into the realm of AI-augmented engineering. By mastering the synergy between Claude Code and Antigravity, you aren't just writing code—you are architecting high-velocity solutions. This is how you transform from a student into a builder who ships.

steps.title: Environment Scaffolding
steps.description: Establish a professional development environment using Antigravity to handle project dependencies and containerization, ensuring your portfolio projects are reproducible.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a professional TypeScript project structure with Docker support, ESLint, and a clear separation between /src and /tests directories.

steps.title: Architecting the Logic Layer
steps.description: Define your application's core business logic using Claude Code to ensure modularity and clean code principles, which are critical for recruiter code reviews.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a modular service layer for an AI-powered task manager that handles user authentication and data persistence.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a service-layer architecture for task management with type-safe interfaces'

steps.title: Implementing AI Integration
steps.description: Integrate LLM capabilities directly into your codebase to demonstrate advanced technical skills beyond standard CRUD applications.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure an API client to connect with Anthropic's Claude API, including error handling and streaming response support.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add-feature 'integrate Claude API with streaming response capabilities'

steps.title: Automated Testing Suite
steps.description: A portfolio project without tests is just a script. Use Claude Code to generate unit and integration tests to prove code quality.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a comprehensive test suite for the task service using Jest, covering edge cases and asynchronous data flow.

steps.title: Deployment Pipeline
steps.description: Configure a CI/CD pipeline so your project is live and accessible, turning a local repo into a verifiable public product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow to run tests and deploy the containerized application to a cloud provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate github-actions pipeline for automated deployment'

pro_tips: Always commit your Claude Code session logs to document your development process; it shows senior engineers how you think.
Use Antigravity to enforce strict linting rules; clean, consistent code is the first thing a tech lead looks for in a portfolio.
Avoid the 'tutorial hell' trap by modifying every generated snippet to solve a unique, real-world edge case.
Structure your README.md to explain the 'Why' behind your architecture, not just the 'What'.
Deploy your project to a custom domain; it significantly increases the perceived value of your portfolio compared to subdomains.
content.next_steps: After completing this project, document your architecture in a blog post and link it directly to your GitHub repo to provide context for recruiters.
content.conversion_block: You now have the tools to build a portfolio that stands out in a crowded market. The difference between a student and a hired engineer is the ability to ship. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
